revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''burial mound''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''burial mound''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:goodbye $1]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:goodbye $1]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: - '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' - '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: - '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] - '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} - '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] - '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: - '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) - '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === - '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' - '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsubura Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]
