The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill||Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:津布良神社 up to rev 98170241
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 津布良神社 |画像= [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]積良[[小字|字]]坪垣内1000 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 2.5 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 54.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 津布良比古命
津布良比賣命 |社格= [[皇大神宮]]末社 |創建= [[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''津布良神社'''（つぶらじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|末社]]。[[水神|水の神]]を祀る神社で、内宮の[[禰宜]]を務め鎮座地周辺を開拓した[[荒木田氏]]との関係が深い伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：65ページ学研パブリッシング（2013）：69ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]積良（つむろ）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：64ページ。鎮座地の「積良」とは[[墓|墳墓]]を意味し、荒木田氏の墳墓が山麓にある。玉城町にある13の内宮摂末社のうちの1社である。内宮の末社16社のうち、[[鴨下神社]]に次ぐ第2位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13 - 14ページ。 3反3畝15歩（≒3,322[[平方メートル|m2]]）ある宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：13ページ社地は大きな森に囲まれている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |title=コース19 玉城ルート2 〜古薫る田園の神々を巡る〜 | publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-08|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1103-2025-45/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course19.html |archivedate=2013-11-03}}。周囲に石積みをめぐらせ、整然としている。[[神明造]]和田（1986）：64ページの[[社殿]]は[[灌漑]]用の池を見下ろす位置に建つ。 [[祭神]]は、津布良比古命（つぶらひこのみこと）と津布良比賣命（つぶらひめのみこと）。両神とも大水上命（おおみなかみのみこと）の子で、田野の水の神とされる。 == 歴史 == 『皇太神宮儀式帳』によれば、[[倭姫命]]が津布良神社を定め、御刀田（[[神田]]）を宛てたという金子（1983）：68ページ。[[室町時代]]には、山宮祭（[[#祭祀|後述]]）の直会が雨天時に津布良神社拝殿で行われたという記録が残っている和田（1986）：66ページ。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]以降、社地すら不明となってしまう。津布良神社が廃絶している間に、地域住民は八王子社（八柱神社）を氏神としてその境内地に建てた櫻井（1991）：276ページ。 明治に入ると、神宮は八柱神社を津布良神社に比定し、津布良神社へ改称するよう求めた。積良村の住民はこれに反発し、もし八柱神社を津布良神社に改称するのであれば、同じ境内にもう1つ社殿を建設し、その社殿を八柱神社として村に任せるよう主張した。[[1873年]]（明治6年）8月に、八柱神社はそのままとし、新しく津布良神社の社殿を境内に建設することで交渉が妥結した。翌[[1874年]]（明治7年）、[[宇治山田神社]]・[[鴨下神社]]・[[大津神社 (伊勢市)|大津神社]]とともに社殿が再興された櫻井（1991）：280 - 281ページ。八柱神社は、[[1907年]]（明治40年）[[11月30日]]に外城田神社（後に東外城田神社）へ合祀された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ |title=東外城田神社 | publisher=三重県神社庁教化委員会 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-08|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1108-1153-24/kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ |archivedate=2013-11-08}}。 [[1896年]]（明治29年）5月に建て替えが行われた。[[2013年]]（平成25年）[[2月1日]]放送の[[三重テレビ放送]]の[[番組]]『[[お伊勢さん (テレビ番組)|お伊勢さん]]』第2回に津布良神社が登場した{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html |title=お伊勢さん | publisher=三重テレビ放送 |language=日本語 |accessdate=2013-11-08|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1108-1141-43/www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html |archivedate=2013-11-08}}。 == 祭祀 == 内宮に合わせて、2月に[[祈年祭]]、6月と12月に[[月次祭]]、10月に[[神嘗祭]]、11月に[[新嘗祭]]が行われる。 かつては荒木田氏を祀る「先祖祭」が行われていた。また神社の南にある3つの谷では「山宮祭」が行われた。山宮祭では荒木田氏が神前に粢（しとぎ、米を水に浸して砕いた物）と木の芽の[[和える|和え物]]を備えていた和田（1986）：65ページ。3つの谷の1つである積良谷には[[サカキ|榊]]の大木を石で囲んだ[[祭壇]]が残っており、荒木田氏による祭祀は途絶えたものの、地域住民によって[[4月3日]]に簡素化した祭が継続されている和田（1986）：65 - 66ページ。 == 交通 == ;公共交通 :付近に[[鉄道駅]]や[[バス停留所]]はなく、鎮座地の積良[[集落]]まで[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[田丸駅]]より南西へ徒歩約1時間（約4.7km）、同線[[外城田駅]]より南へ徒歩約50分（約4.1km）離れている。集落内に「積良」と書かれた青い[[看板]]があり、この看板のある交差点を南下し少し進むと、左手に見える高台が津布良神社である。伊勢市観光協会によれば、神社へ至る道は分かりづらいという。[[1980年代]]には[[伊勢市駅]]から玉城町の野中行きの[[路線バス]]が運行され、「幸神」[[バス停留所|バス停]]が最寄りの停留所であった。 ;自家用車 :[[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約11分（約3.3[[キロメートル|km]]）。神社前の道は未舗装である。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 和田年弥（1986）"津布良神社"『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』（[[谷川健一]] 編、[[白水社]]、1986年3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X）:64-66. == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_06.jpg 津布良神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html 津布良神社（つぶらじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:つふらしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]] {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District, Mie|qq=}}, Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Tsubura Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Setsumatsusha
revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]). It is dedicated to the '''deities of water''' and has strong ties to the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, a family that historically served as '''negis (禰宜)''' of Ise Grand Shrine and contributed to the development of the surrounding area.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 65Gakken Publishing, 2013, p. 69 == Overview == Tsubura Shrine is located in '''Tsumuro, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture'''.Ise Culture Publishing, 2008, p. 64 The name "Tsumuro" (積良) means '''Kofun''', referring to the nearby '''Arakida family tombs''' located at the foot of the mountains. It is '''one of 13 Ise Grand Shrine subsidiary shrines in Tamaki Town''' and '''Ranks second among the 16 Inner Shrine subsidiary shrines''', following {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, pp. 13-14 The shrine grounds, spanning '''3,322 square meters''', are surrounded by '''dense forest'''.Ujiyamada City Office, 1929, p. 13 The area is enclosed by '''stone walls''' and is well maintained. The '''shrine hall, built in the Shinmei-zukuri style''', overlooks an '''irrigation pond'''. The '''enshrined deities''' are: * '''Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)''' * '''Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命)''' These '''siblings''' are children of '''Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)''' and are worshiped as '''gods of agricultural water'''. == History == According to the '''Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō (皇太神宮儀式帳)''', '''Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto''' enshrined Tsubura Shrine and designated its sacred rice fields (御刀田, Mikadota).Kaneko, 1983, p. 68 During the '''Muromachi period''', the '''Yamamiyasai festival''' (山宮祭) was held, with records indicating that '''rainy-day ceremonies took place at the Tsubura Shrine prayer hall'''.Wada, 1986, p. 66 During the '''Sengoku period''', the shrine's exact location became unknown.Wada, 1986, p. 64 In the interim, the '''Hachioji Shrine (八柱神社)''' was established by local residents and functioned as their '''Ujigami (氏神, local tutelary deity)'''.Sakurai, 1991, p. 276 After the '''Meiji Restoration''', the Ise Grand Shrine administration '''identified Hachioji Shrine as Tsubura Shrine''' and proposed renaming it accordingly. However, the '''residents of Tsumuro resisted''' and '''negotiated to maintain Hachioji Shrine within the same precincts''', leading to the '''construction of a new Tsubura Shrine hall in 1873 (Meiji 6).''' The shrine was formally rebuilt '''in 1874 (Meiji 7)''', along with '''{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}'''.Sakurai, 1991, pp. 280-281 In '''1907 (Meiji 40),''' Hachioji Shrine was '''merged with Tojōda Shrine (later renamed Higashi-Tojōda Shrine).'''{{Cite web|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%E6%9D%B1%E5%A4%96%E5%9F%8E%E7%94%B0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/|title=Higashi-Tojōda Shrine|publisher=Mie Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2013-11-08}} A '''reconstruction''' took place in '''1896 (Meiji 29).''' More recently, the shrine was featured in '''Episode 2''' of the '''Mie TV program "O-Ise-san"''', aired on '''February 1, 2013 (Heisei 25).'''{{Cite web|url=http://www.mietv.com/oisesan/yotei_02.html|title=O-Ise-san|publisher=Mie TV|accessdate=2013-11-08}} == Festivals == Tsubura Shrine follows the festival calendar of the '''Inner Shrine''', hosting: * '''February:''' [[Kinensai|Kinen-sai (祈年祭)]] * '''June & December:''' {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|en:draft|lt=Tsukinami-sai (月次祭)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} * '''October:''' [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai Festival (神嘗祭)]] * '''November:''' [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai Festival (新嘗祭)]] Historically, a '''"Senzosai" (Ancestor Festival)''' was held in honor of the '''Arakida clan'''. Additionally, the '''Yamamiyasai Festival''' was conducted in three valleys south of the shrine. This ritual included offerings of '''shito (粢, water-soaked crushed rice)''' and '''tree bud salads'''.Wada, 1986, p. 65 One of these valleys, '''Tsumuro Valley''', still retains a '''sacred tree altar''', surrounded by stones. Although the '''Arakida clan’s ceremonies ended''', local residents continue a '''simplified version''' of the festival '''on April 3'''.Wada, 1986, pp. 65-66 == Access == === Public Transport === Tsubura Shrine '''does not have nearby train stations or bus stops'''. The closest railway stations are: * '''Tamaki Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 1-hour walk (4.7 km southwest) * '''Tojōda Station (JR Sangu Line)''' – 50-minute walk (4.1 km south) The entrance to '''Tsumuro Village''' features a '''blue sign marked "積良"''', indicating the intersection leading south to Tsubura Shrine. The '''Ise City Tourism Association''' warns that '''navigation can be difficult'''. === By Car === * '''From Tamaki IC (Ise Expressway):''' Approx. '''11 minutes (3.3 km)''' * '''No parking is available near the shrine.''' == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0703.html Tsubura Shrine (伊勢神宮崇敬会)] * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ Tamaki Town Shrine Guide] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津布良神社|version=98170241|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Tsubura Shrine]] [[ja:津布良神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

{{infobox shinto shrine | Name = Tsubura Shrine (津布良神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Tsubura-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], Mie Prefecture | Address = 1000 Tsuboguchi, Tsumuro, Tamaki, Watarai District, Mie Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|2.5|N|136|35|54.3|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsubura Shrine}} | Deities = Tsuburahiko-no-Mikoto (津布良比古命)
Tsuburahime-no-Mikoto (津布良比賣命) | type = {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subsidiary Shrine (末社) | Founded = Before 804 CE (Enryaku 23) | Main Hall Style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Associated Festivals = [[Kannamesai Festival]] }} '''Tsubura Shrine''' (津布良神社) is a subsidiary shrine (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine of [[Ise Grand Shrine]]).
